expectations.text: Uses useWriteContracts hook (not useSendCalls or custom encoding)
expectations.evidence: Uses useSendCalls from wagmi (lower-level) instead of useWriteContracts from wagmi/experimental. Requires manual encodeFunctionData and a hacky MAX_CONTRACTS=5 padded hook pattern

expectations.text: Uses useCapabilities for wallet EIP-5792 support detection
expectations.evidence: useBatchCallsCapabilities hook wraps useCapabilities from wagmi to check atomicBatch support

expectations.text: Uses useShowCallsStatus for batch transaction status display
expectations.evidence: No useShowCallsStatus usage. No way to show batch status in wallet's native UI after submission

expectations.text: Provides graceful fallback for wallets without EIP-5792 support
expectations.evidence: Only one 'Approve + Transfer (Batch)' button. No individual transaction fallback path. If wallet doesn't support EIP-5792, user has no alternative

expectations.text: Batch button conditionally disabled when wallet doesn't support EIP-5792
expectations.evidence: disabled={isBatching || !connectedAddress || !recipientAddress || !transferAmount} — does NOT check isSupported from useBatchCallsCapabilities

expectations.text: ERC20 smart contract with approve+transfer pattern created
expectations.evidence: BatchToken.sol created with mint(), approve/transferFrom via OpenZeppelin ERC20

expectations.text: Frontend page with batch UI created
expectations.evidence: /batch-transfer page with wallet capabilities badges, token info, batch form, how-it-works section

expectations.text: Uses SE-2 scaffold hooks for contract interaction
expectations.evidence: useScaffoldReadContract, useScaffoldWriteContract, useDeployedContractInfo all used correctly

expectations.text: No new npm dependencies needed (wagmi already has EIP-5792 hooks)
expectations.evidence: No new dependencies added — uses existing wagmi hooks
